rawContent: Task: 419-260405-054539 | Outcome: completed | Observation: Task 419-260405-054539 (JWT auth redesign with RBAC, rerun from April 5) completed all 7 pipeline stages with 0 retries across taskify/plan/build/verify/review, 1 retry on review-fix, and 0 on ship. Verify stage reported FAIL due to 1 test error (src/middleware/rbac.test.ts) while review passed with verdict PASS. Notably, the ship.md artifact shows 'Ship stage skipped — local mode, no issue number' rather than the normal ship output seen in prior runs, indicating this rerun did not produce an actual PR shipment despite ship stage reporting completed. This differs from the 8 prior consecutive runs where verify FAIL still proceeded to ship. | Pattern: This run partially matches the persistent verify-to-ship gate flaw seen across 8 prior runs (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842, 844, and two 419-reruns), where verify stage FAIL does not block pipeline progression. However, this run diverges: ship stage was explicitly skipped rather than completing a shipment, and review-fix had 1 retry indicating it required additional work to pass review. | Suggestion: Investigate why ship stage produced 'skipped' output despite reporting completed with 0 retries, and confirm whether the JWT auth redesign actually shipped or was halted at the ship gate. The skipped output suggests either a different ship gate implementation for rerun mode or a distinct halving mechanism triggered by local mode. | Flaw: ship — Ship stage reports completed with 0 retries but outputs 'skipped — local mode, no issue number', creating ambiguity about whether the JWT auth redesign actually shipped or was blocked. The ship.md artifact structure differs from normal shipped runs, suggesting rerun mode may have different ship gate behavior that needs verification. | Stages: taskify:completed, plan:completed, build:completed, verify:completed, review:completed, review-fix:completed, ship:completed
